in a previous life in a newsroom, we would often report on ISIS beheading videos, and there was a lot of thinking around not simply re-airing their actions, being tools for their propaganda of the deed, no such thinking over the elevation of imagery from earlier this week

so maybe taking the energy to express how SURPRISING and UNPRECEDENTED things are is a waste of everyone's energy, maybe you should look to the people who weren't surprised, who were warning of this for ages, and fucking LISTEN TO THEM

If I had coworkers who reacted to new unexpected problems by freaking out, from like Wednesday to Saturday, "oh my god it's so scary!" "oh my god look what they did!" instead of actually sitting back and planning concrete actions that WE OURSELVES can take, we would all be fired
